strategies.description: The strategy exploits a negative cross-sectional relation between a stock's maximum daily return over the past month (MAX) and its expected return in the following month. Stocks with the highest MAX (lottery-like payoffs) are overvalued and subsequently underperform, while those with the lowest MAX outperform. A long-short portfolio that buys the lowest MAX decile and shorts the highest MAX decile generates significant raw and risk-adjusted returns.
strategies.data_source: CRSP, COMPUSTAT, Kenneth French's Data Library
strategies.universe_selection_criteria: NYSE/AMEX/NASDAQ stocks; robustness checks exclude stocks with price < $5, exclude AMEX/NASDAQ, exclude smallest NYSE size quintile (microcaps); book-to-market winsorized at 0.5% and 99.5%

indicators.name: Maximum Daily Return (MAX)
indicators.formula: For each stock i in month t, compute the maximum of daily returns over all trading days in that month.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily close prices for stock i over month t. Build daily return series R_{i,d} = (close_d - close_{d-1}) / close_{d-1}. Output is the maximum of these daily returns, a scalar per stock-month.

indicators.name: Average of Five Highest Daily Returns (MAX(5))
indicators.formula: For each stock i in month t, compute the average of the five highest daily returns over all trading days in that month.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily close prices for stock i over month t. Build daily return series R_{i,d} = (close_d - close_{d-1}) / close_{d-1}. Sort returns descending, take the top 5, compute their average. Output is a scalar per stock-month.

indicators.name: Averaged MAX over Past 3 Months
indicators.formula: For each stock i, compute the maximum daily return in each of the past 3 months, then average those three values.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily close prices for stock i over the past 3 months. For each month, compute the maximum daily return. Then average the three monthly maxima. Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Market Beta (Scholes-Williams)
indicators.formula: Market beta estimated using the Scholes-Williams (1977) / Dimson (1979) method with one lead and one lag of the market return.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily returns for stock i and the market index over the estimation period. Regress stock excess return on market excess return (contemporaneous), market excess return lagged by 1 day, and market excess return led by 1 day. Sum the three slope coefficients and divide by (1 + 2*autocorrelation of market returns). Output is a scalar beta per stock.

indicators.name: Market Capitalization (SIZE)
indicators.formula: Natural logarithm of market capitalization at the end of month t-1.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are month-end close price and shares outstanding for stock i. Multiply to get market cap, then take natural log. Output is a scalar per stock at each month-end.

indicators.name: Book-to-Market Ratio (BM)
indicators.formula: Book value of equity divided by market value of equity at the end of December of the previous year. Winsorized at the 0.5% and 99.5% levels.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are book value of equity from the latest fiscal year ending in the prior calendar year, and market cap at end of December of the previous year. Divide book by market cap. Winsorize the ratio at the 0.5% and 99.5% levels across all stocks. Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Momentum (MOM)
indicators.formula: Cumulative return over the 11 months ending 2 months prior to the current month (months t-12 to t-2).
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are monthly close prices for stock i. Compute monthly returns. Skip the most recent month (t-1). Compound returns from month t-12 to t-2 inclusive. Output is a scalar cumulative return per stock.

indicators.name: Short-term Reversal (REV)
indicators.formula: Return in the previous month (month t-1).
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are monthly close prices for stock i. Compute monthly return for the most recent month (t-1). Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Illiquidity (ILLIQ)
indicators.formula: Ratio of absolute monthly stock return to its dollar trading volume.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are monthly return and monthly dollar trading volume for stock i. Take absolute value of monthly return, divide by dollar volume (price * shares traded). Output is a scalar per stock-month.

indicators.name: Idiosyncratic Volatility (IVOL)
indicators.formula: Standard deviation of daily residuals from a single-factor market model regression (excess stock return on excess market return) within the month.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily returns for stock i, risk-free rate, and market index return over month t. Compute excess returns (stock return - risk-free rate, market return - risk-free rate). Regress stock excess return on market excess return. Compute standard deviation of the residuals from this regression. Output is a scalar per stock-month.

indicators.name: Negative of Minimum Daily Return (MIN)
indicators.formula: Negative of the minimum daily return within a month.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily close prices for stock i over month t. Build daily return series. Find the minimum daily return, then multiply by -1. Output is a scalar per stock-month.

indicators.name: Total Skewness (TSKEW)
indicators.formula: Skewness of daily returns within a year.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily returns for stock i over the past year. Compute mean and standard deviation of daily returns. For each day, compute cubed standardized return, then average across all days. Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Systematic Skewness / Co-skewness (SSKEW)
indicators.formula: Coefficient on squared market return from a regression of stock returns on market return and squared market return.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily returns for stock i and market index over the past year. Regress stock daily return on market daily return and squared market daily return. The coefficient on the squared market term is the systematic skewness. Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Idiosyncratic Skewness (ISKEW)
indicators.formula: Skewness of daily residuals from the SSKEW regression.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are daily returns for stock i and market index over the past year. First run the SSKEW regression to obtain residuals. Then compute the skewness of those residuals. Output is a scalar per stock.

indicators.name: Expected Total Skewness (E(TSKEW))
indicators.formula: Fitted values from a cross-sectional regression of TSKEW on lagged TSKEW and control variables.
indicators.executable_explanation: Inputs are lagged TSKEW and control variables (e.g., SIZE, BM, MOM, IVOL, etc.) for all stocks. Run a cross-sectional regression of current TSKEW on lagged values. The fitted values from this regression are the expected total skewness. Output is a scalar per stock.

logic_pipeline.description: Filter stocks based on price and size to exclude micro-caps and low-priced stocks
logic_pipeline.expression: Keep stocks with price >= $5 and market capitalization above the smallest two NYSE size deciles (i.e., exclude micro-caps).
logic_pipeline.executable_explanation: Inputs are price (scalar per stock) and market cap (scalar per stock); compute boolean filter; output boolean mask for stock universe.

logic_pipeline.description: Calculate MAX indicator: maximum daily return over the past one month
logic_pipeline.expression: MAX_i,t = max(R_i,d) for d = 1 to D_t, where R_i,d is the daily return on stock i on day d, and D_t is the number of trading days in month t.
logic_pipeline.executable_explanation: Input is daily returns series for each stock over the past month; compute the maximum value; output scalar MAX per stock.

logic_pipeline.description: Sort all stocks into deciles based on MAX indicator from the previous month
logic_pipeline.expression: Each month, sort all filtered stocks into 10 decile portfolios based on MAX. Decile 1 = lowest MAX, Decile 10 = highest MAX.
logic_pipeline.executable_explanation: Input is MAX scalar per stock; rank and assign to deciles Q1 (lowest) to Q10 (highest); output categorical label per stock.

logic_pipeline.description: Generate long-short trade signal based on MAX decile assignment
logic_pipeline.expression: IF max_decile == 'Q1' THEN 'long_target' (buy low MAX stocks); IF max_decile == 'Q10' THEN 'short_target' (sell high MAX stocks); ELSE 'hold' (no position).
logic_pipeline.executable_explanation: Input is MAX decile label per stock; apply conditional logic; output categorical trade signal (long_target, short_target, hold).

execution_plan.description: Monthly long-short portfolio based on MAX decile sorts from previous month

steps.description: Assign equal weights to all stocks within each decile portfolio, then combine long and short legs with equal total notional exposure
steps.expression: For each stock in decile 1 (long leg): weight = 1/N_long; for each stock in decile 10 (short leg): weight = -1/N_short; all other stocks: weight = 0. Long and short legs are each scaled to 100% gross exposure (i.e., sum of absolute weights = 2).
steps.executable_explanation: At month-end t-1, after trade_signal is generated (decile labels), assign equal weights to stocks in decile 1 (long) and decile 10 (short). Weights are applied at the close of month t-1 (or open of month t) and held for one month. Positive weights = long, negative weights = short.

position_sizing.executable_explanation: Quantile-based equal weighting within each decile, with long-short construction. The logic_pipeline output (max_decile) is used to assign stocks to portfolios; no further signal scaling is applied.

execution_plan.executable_explanation: This plan consumes the trade_signal (decile labels) from the logic_pipeline. Positive weights (long) are assigned to decile 1 stocks, negative weights (short) to decile 10 stocks. All other stocks receive zero weight. The portfolio is rebalanced monthly.

risk_management: No explicit stop-loss, position limit, sector exposure cap, or drawdown constraint is specified in the paper.
The paper applies price filters (exclude stocks below $5/share) and size filters (exclude microcaps) as robustness checks, but these are not part of the primary strategy's risk management rules.
Winsorization of MAX at the 99th percentile is used in robustness checks, not as a live risk rule.
strategies.executable_explanation: At the end of each month t-1, for each stock in the filtered universe, compute MAX as the maximum daily return over month t-1, assign deciles cross-sectionally, then at the close of month t-1 (or open of month t) go long on decile 1 (lowest MAX) and short on decile 10 (highest MAX), holding for one month until the next rebalance.
strategies.risk_management_executable_explanation: No explicit risk management rules (stop-loss, position limits, drawdown caps) are specified in the paper. The strategy relies solely on the factor-based portfolio construction and monthly rebalancing.

hitl_resolutions.detail: Equal-weight within each decile portfolio. Long and short legs each at 100% notional exposure (200% gross). Dollar-neutral (equal notional).
Orders placed at the last bar of month t-1 execute at the open of the first bar of month t. This avoids lookahead bias by ensuring signal calculation uses only data up to month t-1 end.
No stop-loss, position limits, drawdown constraints, or volatility scaling. Strategy matches the paper exactly — relies solely on factor-based portfolio construction and monthly rebalancing.
